title: Tragedy in Kenya: Mother Finds Son After Ebola Center Protests
subtitle: As tensions rise over a planned US quarantine facility, families demand answers following deadly clashes.

summary.en: A mother in Nanyuki, Kenya, has discovered the body of her 17-year-old son following violent protests against a planned US Ebola quarantine center. The teenager was caught in clashes between police and demonstrators who fear the facility poses public health risks. While the government defends the project as a humanitarian necessity, local groups argue that the government has lacked transparency. Experts note that successful disease response requires working closely with local communities rather than ignoring their concerns. The incident marks the third death linked to the ongoing unrest surrounding the construction site.
summary.zh: 肯亞南尤基鎮的一位母親在反對美國伊波拉隔離中心的暴力抗議後，發現了她十七歲兒子的遺體。這名青少年在警方與擔心該設施構成公共衛生風險的示威者之間的衝突中喪生。儘管政府將該計畫辯護為人道主義的必要措施，但當地團體認為政府缺乏透明度。專家指出，成功的防疫工作需要與當地社區密切合作，而非忽視他們的擔憂。這起事件是圍繞該施工地點持續騷亂中發生的第三起死亡案件。

paragraphs.en: A grieving mother in the Kenyan town of Nanyuki has finally found her missing son, two days after he disappeared during violent protests. Lucy Kagure discovered the body of 17-year-old Sylvester Muigai Ndung'u at a local mortuary, where he was listed as an unidentified male. The teenager had been killed on Tuesday during a clash between police and protesters who were demonstrating against a planned US Ebola quarantine center—a facility designed to isolate people who might be sick with the virus—at a nearby military base.
paragraphs.zh: 在肯亞南尤基鎮（Nanyuki），一位悲痛欲絕的母親在兒子於暴力抗議中失蹤兩天後，終於找到了他。露西·卡古雷（Lucy Kagure）在當地停屍間發現了十七歲兒子西爾維斯特·穆蓋·恩東古（Sylvester Muigai Ndung'u）的遺體，當時他被列為身分不明的男性。這名青少年是在週二死於警方與抗議民眾的衝突，當時民眾正針對附近軍事基地計畫興建的美國伊波拉病毒隔離中心進行示威，該設施旨在隔離可能感染該病毒的人員。

paragraphs.en: According to the BBC, the young man had left his home to collect a school uniform when he became caught in the middle of the unrest. His mother described the horrific state in which she found him, noting that his clothes were covered in blood and his head had been severely injured. While witnesses told the BBC that the teenager had been shot in the head, a local police commander, Daniel Kitavi, stated that authorities are still waiting for a medical examination to confirm the exact cause of death. Family members have suggested that a tear-gas canister, rather than a bullet, may have caused the fatal injury.
paragraphs.zh: 根據英國廣播公司（BBC）報導，這名年輕人當時是出門領取學校制服，卻意外捲入騷亂之中。他的母親描述了她發現兒子時那慘不忍睹的景象，並指出他的衣服沾滿鮮血，頭部受到重創。儘管目擊者向 BBC 表示這名青少年頭部中彈，但當地警官丹尼爾·基塔維（Daniel Kitavi）表示，當局仍在等待驗屍報告以確認確切死因。家屬則認為，造成致命傷的可能不是子彈，而是催淚瓦斯罐。

paragraphs.en: This tragedy is the latest in a series of events that have turned the town of Nanyuki into a place of deep conflict. Muigai is the third person to die during protests against the 50-bed isolation unit. The facility is intended to treat US citizens who may have been exposed to Ebola in the Democratic Republic of Congo. However, the plan has faced strong opposition from local residents who worry about the risk of the disease spreading into their communities. Many citizens have also criticized the government for a lack of transparency regarding the project.
paragraphs.zh: 這場悲劇是導致南尤基鎮陷入嚴重衝突的一連串事件中的最新一起。穆蓋是第三位在反對這座五十床隔離單位的抗議活動中喪生的人。該設施旨在治療可能在剛果民主共和國接觸過伊波拉病毒的美國公民。然而，這項計畫面臨當地居民的強烈反對，他們擔心疾病傳播到社區的風險。許多市民也批評政府在該計畫上缺乏透明度。

paragraphs.en: Despite a recent High Court order to stop construction, satellite images seen by the BBC indicate that work at the airbase has continued. President William Ruto has defended the project, arguing that refusing the US request to build the center would be "inhuman." He urged citizens and politicians to avoid making the situation a political issue, describing the threat of Ebola as a very serious matter. Meanwhile, the Kenya Human Rights Commission has accused the police of using excessive force, including the use of live ammunition and making unfair arrests during the demonstrations.
paragraphs.zh: 儘管高等法院近期下令停止施工，但 BBC 取得的衛星影像顯示，空軍基地的工程仍在持續。肯亞總統威廉·魯托（William Ruto）為該計畫辯護，稱拒絕美國興建該中心的請求是「不人道」的行為。他敦促市民與政治人物避免將此事政治化，並形容伊波拉病毒的威脅是非常嚴肅的問題。與此同時，肯亞人權委員會（Kenya Human Rights Commission）指控警方在示威期間過度使用武力，包括使用實彈及進行不公正的逮捕。

paragraphs.en: Beyond the specific situation in Kenya, experts in global health emphasize that managing an Ebola outbreak is a complex challenge that goes beyond just building facilities. According to STAT News, Ebola is often called a "disease of compassion" because it tends to spread through the care people provide to sick family members and through traditional burial rites. When health authorities fail to respect these deep-seated cultural and religious traditions, it often leads to mistrust, fear, and even attacks on medical centers. Research has shown that when communities feel their traditions are ignored, they are less likely to follow public health advice.
paragraphs.zh: 除了肯亞的特定情況外，全球衛生專家強調，管理伊波拉疫情是一項複雜的挑戰，不僅僅是興建設施而已。根據 STAT 新聞網（STAT News）報導，伊波拉常被稱為「同情心的疾病」，因為它往往透過人們照顧生病的家人以及傳統葬禮儀式而傳播。當衛生當局未能尊重這些根深蒂固的文化與宗教傳統時，往往會導致不信任、恐懼，甚至引發對醫療中心的攻擊。研究顯示，當社區感到其傳統被忽視時，他們較不願意遵循公共衛生建議。

paragraphs.en: In previous outbreaks, such as the one in West Africa in 2014, initial efforts to force people to change their burial practices were unsuccessful and sometimes dangerous. It was only when health organizations began working with religious leaders to create safe, respectful burial protocols that they saw a significant improvement in cooperation. STAT News noted that these leaders act as a bridge between medical science and the spiritual needs of the community. Without this kind of engagement, emergency responses often overlook the social factors that cause a disease to spread.
paragraphs.zh: 在過去的疫情中，例如二〇一四年的西非疫情，初期強迫民眾改變葬禮習俗的努力不僅失敗，有時甚至會造成危險。直到衛生組織開始與宗教領袖合作，制定安全且尊重的葬禮規範後，才看到合作意願顯著提升。STAT 新聞網指出，這些領袖在醫學科學與社區精神需求之間扮演了橋樑的角色。若缺乏這種參與，緊急應變措施往往會忽略導致疾病傳播的社會因素。

paragraphs.en: For the family of Sylvester Muigai Ndung'u, the broader political and medical debates offer little comfort. His mother, who worked hard as a single parent to support her son’s education, is now calling for justice. She told the BBC that she wants to understand how such a peaceful boy, who dreamed of becoming a priest, could lose his life in such a violent way. As the standoff between the government and the protesters continues, the people of Nanyuki remain stuck in a dangerous and uncertain situation, waiting for answers about both the safety of their community and the loss of their loved ones.
paragraphs.zh: 對於西爾維斯特·穆蓋·恩東古的家人來說，更廣泛的政治與醫學辯論無法帶來多少慰藉。他的母親身為單親家長，辛苦工作以支持兒子的教育，如今她呼籲伸張正義。她告訴 BBC，她想知道像這樣一個夢想成為神職人員、個性溫和的孩子，為何會以如此暴力的方式喪生。隨著政府與抗議民眾之間的對峙持續，南尤基鎮的人民仍深陷危險且不確定的處境中，等待著關於社區安全與失去摯親的答案。

keyTerms.definitionEn: To be recorded or included on an official register or document.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作名詞（清單），這裡作動詞的被動用法，指身分被登記。文中：Lucy Kagure discovered the body of 17-year-old Sylvester Muigai Ndung'u at a local mortuary, where he was listed as an unidentified male.

keyTerms.definitionEn: Unable to move or escape from a difficult or unpleasant situation.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作動詞 stick 的過去式，這裡作形容詞描述狀態。文中：As the standoff between the government and the protesters continues, the people of Nanyuki remain stuck in a dangerous and uncertain situation, waiting for answers about both the safety of their community and the loss of their loved ones.

keyTerms.definitionEn: Something that provides a connection between two different groups or ideas.
keyTerms.definitionZh: 橋樑（比喻連接不同群體或觀點的事物）。
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作名詞（實體橋樑），這裡用作比喻，指溝通的媒介。文中：STAT News noted that these leaders act as a bridge between medical science and the spiritual needs of the community.

sources.name: BBC News
sources.title: Mother finds body of missing son two days after Kenya's Ebola quarantine centre protests

sources.name: STAT News
sources.title: Opinion: Ebola outbreak response requires engaging with religious traditions and leaders

previousArticles.title: Deadly Protests Continue in Kenya Over US Ebola Center
previousArticles.summaryEn: A demonstrator has died during a protest in Nanyuki, Kenya, against a planned US Ebola quarantine center. The facility is intended to treat Americans exposed to the virus in the Democratic Republic of Congo. Despite a court order to halt construction, work at the site has reportedly continued. President William Ruto has defended the project, citing the need to support a long-term partner. Local residents remain concerned about the risk of the virus entering their community.

previousArticles.title: Protests Turn Deadly in Kenya Over US Ebola Quarantine Center
previousArticles.summaryEn: Violent protests have erupted in the Kenyan town of Nanyuki over a new quarantine facility for US citizens. The center is designed to hold Americans exposed to the Ebola virus in the Democratic Republic of Congo. While the Kenyan government supports the project as a partnership with a long-term ally, local residents fear the virus could enter their country. A high court has attempted to stop construction, but work continues despite the legal challenges. Experts and activists remain divided on whether the facility truly improves global health security.

quiz.question: What is the primary reason local residents in Nanyuki are protesting against the new medical facility?

options.A: They believe the facility is meant for local citizens rather than US personnel.
options.B: They fear the Ebola virus could escape the facility and infect their community.
options.C: They are angry that the construction project has been delayed by the High Court.
options.D: They object to the military base being used for civilian medical purposes.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。根據文章第三段，當地居民強烈反對該設施，是因為擔心傳染病擴散到他們的社區（worry about the risk of the disease spreading into their communities）。選項 A 與文意相反，設施是為了美國公民；選項 C 雖然提到法院命令，但這是政府與法院的衝突，而非居民抗議的初衷；選項 D 雖然提到軍事基地，但抗議的核心是 Ebola 病毒的傳染風險，而非基地的用途。

quiz.question: Based on the information provided, why might the local community's relationship with the government be deteriorating?

options.A: The government has officially confirmed that a tear-gas canister killed the teenager.
options.B: The government has successfully stopped all construction at the airbase as ordered.
options.C: The government is ignoring legal rulings and using force against citizens who have legitimate health concerns.
options.D: The government has refused to provide any medical treatment to the families of the victims.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。綜合文章第三、四段可知，政府在法院下令停止施工後仍持續作業，且人權委員會指控警方在抗議中使用過度武力（如實彈），這顯示政府無視法律程序並以暴力對待表達健康疑慮的民眾，導致雙方關係惡化。選項 A 錯誤，官方尚未確認死因；選項 B 與文中衛星影像顯示施工持續的事實矛盾；選項 D 文中並未提及。

quiz.question: In the fifth paragraph, what does the author mean by calling Ebola a 'disease of compassion'?

options.A: It is a disease that makes people feel deep sympathy for the sick.
options.B: It is a disease that spreads through the acts of care and love for family members.
options.C: It is a disease that can only be cured by showing kindness to the infected.
options.D: It is a disease that health organizations treat with a focus on emotional support.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。文中提到 Ebola 被稱為「compassion 的疾病」，是因為它往往透過人們照顧生病家人的行為（care people provide to sick family members）以及傳統葬禮儀式傳播。這裡的 compassion 指的是照顧病患的愛心與行為，卻反而成為病毒傳播的途徑。選項 A、C、D 均誤解了作者將「compassion」與「傳播途徑」連結的語境含義。

quiz.question: What is the central message of the article?

options.A: The US government is solely responsible for the political unrest in Nanyuki.
options.B: Ebola outbreaks can be easily managed if military bases are used for isolation.
options.C: The death of Sylvester Muigai is a minor incident in the global fight against Ebola.
options.D: Effective disease management requires respecting local social and cultural traditions, not just building infrastructure.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 D。文章透過 Nanyuki 的悲劇與西非疫情的歷史經驗，強調僅靠興建隔離設施（硬體）是不夠的，若不尊重當地的宗教與文化傳統（軟體），往往會導致民眾不信任與防疫失敗。選項 A 過度簡化政府責任；選項 B 與文章觀點相反；選項 C 輕視了該悲劇在文中作為探討社會衝突與防疫失敗案例的重要性。
